subject: External monitor not detected after dock reconnect
description: Laptop on USB-C dock; external display worked yesterday; today only built-in screen lights up.

ts_steps.title: Power-cycle dock
ts_steps.action: Unplug dock AC and Thunderbolt cable from Mac; wait 15 s.

ts_steps.title: Reconnect order
ts_steps.action: Plug dock AC until LED stable, then single TB/USB-C cable to Mac on the port documented for display bandwidth.

ts_steps.title: Direct cable test
ts_steps.action: If available, bypass dock with certified USB-C to DisplayPort cable Mac-to-monitor.

ts_steps.title: Detect Displays
ts_steps.action: System Settings → Displays → hold Option → Detect Displays. Try Gather Windows if dialog off-screen.

ts_steps.title: Mirror and resolution
ts_steps.action: Toggle mirror extend; pick Default resolution or safe scaled mode; avoid exotic refresh on cheap adapters.

ts_steps.title: Lid and clamshell
ts_steps.action: Open/close lid once; ensure AC power for clamshell per Apple guidance.

ts_steps.title: Reduce dock load
ts_steps.action: Disconnect non-essential USB devices from dock; some docks drop DP alt mode under load.

ts_steps.title: Sleep wake
ts_steps.action: Apple menu → Sleep then wake; or lock and unlock.

ts_steps.title: Firmware and cable
ts_steps.action: Update dock firmware from OEM; swap TB3/TB4 certified cable.

ts_steps.title: Vendor ticket data
ts_steps.action: Collect Mac model, macOS build, dock model, monitor EDID if known for escalation.

internal_information: USB-C DP tunneling; flaky dongles; dock firmware. No discrete-GPU Intel narrative.
